description: Adds a script which would be evaluated whenever the page is navigated. @mcp
help:
Adds a script which would be evaluated whenever the page is navigated. @mcp

The script is evaluated after the document was created but before any of its scripts were run. This is useful to amend the JavaScript environment, e.g. to seed [Math.random].

This function should be invoked before a navigation, usually in an onWillNavigate event handler.

description: Blocks resource URLs from loading. @mcp
help: Blocks resource URLs from loading. @mcp

description: Opens the specified URL in the web driver. @mcp
help:
Opens the specified URL in the web driver. @mcp

This function navigates the web driver to the provided URL and waits for the navigation to complete. It is a suspend function, meaning it can be used within coroutines for asynchronous execution.

Example usage: ```kotlin driver.open("https://www.example.com") ```

description: Navigates current page to the given URL. @mcp
help:
Navigates current page to the given URL. @mcp

```kotlin driver.navigate("https://www.example.com") driver.waitForNavigation() ```

description: Navigates current page to the given URL. @mcp
help:
Navigates current page to the given URL. @mcp

```kotlin val entry = NavigateEntry("https://www.example.com?timestamp=11712067353", pageUrl = "https://www.example.com") driver.navigate(entry) driver.waitForNavigation() ```

description: Reloads the current page. @mcp
help:
Reloads the current page. @mcp

This method should trigger a reload of the current page, similar to how a user would refresh the page in a browser.

description: Navigates the browser to the previous page in the navigation history. @mcp
help:
Navigates the browser to the previous page in the navigation history. @mcp

This method is expected to use the browser's navigation history to move back to the previous page. It should handle any exceptions that may occur during the navigation process.

description: Navigates the browser to the next page in the navigation history. @mcp
help:
Navigates the browser to the next page in the navigation history. @mcp

This method is expected to use the browser's navigation history to move forward to the next page. It should handle any exceptions that may occur during the navigation process.

description: Returns a string representing the current URL that the browser is looking at. @mcp
help:
Returns a string representing the current URL that the browser is looking at. @mcp

The current url is always the main frame's `document.documentURI` if the browser succeed to return it, and is displayed in the browser's address bar.

If the browser failed to return a proper url, returns the passed in url to navigate, just like a real user enter a url in the address bar but the browser failed to load the page.

NOTE: the url can be non-standard, for example:

about:blank chrome://newtab chrome://settings

see [ai.platon.cdt.kt.protocol.types.page.NavigationEntry.userTypedURL]

description: The URL read-only property of the Document interface returns the document location as a string. @mcp
help:
The URL read-only property of the Document interface returns the document location as a string. @mcp

This property equals to javascript `document.URL`. The `document.URL` property returns the same value. The `document.documentURI` property can be used on any document types, while the `document.URL` property can only be used on HTML documents.

description: Returns the document location as a string. @mcp
help:
Returns the document location as a string. @mcp

This property equals to javascript `document.documentURI`.

The `document.URL` property which returns the same value. The `document.documentURI` property can be used on any document types, while the `document.URL` property can only be used on HTML documents.

description: Returns the document's baseURI. @mcp
help:
Returns the document's baseURI. @mcp

The baseURI is a property of Node, it's the absolute base URL of the document containing the node. A baseURI is used to resolve relative URLs.

The base URL is determined as follows: 1. By default, the base URL is the location of the document (as determined by window.location). 2. If the document has an `<base>` element, its href attribute is used.

description: The referrer property returns the URI of the page that linked to this page. @mcp
help:
The referrer property returns the URI of the page that linked to this page. @mcp

The value is an empty string if the user navigated to the page directly (not through a link, but, for example, by using a bookmark).

Inside an <iframe>, the referrer will initially be set to the same value as the href of the parent window's Window.location.

description: Returns the source of the last loaded page. @mcp
help:
Returns the source of the last loaded page. @mcp

If the page has been modified after loading (for example, by JavaScript) there is no guarantee that the returned text is that of the modified page.

PageSource and outerHTML:

- pageSource: returns document HTML markup, will support non-HTML document - outerHTML: returns document HTML markup, for HTML document only

```kotlin val pageSource = driver.pageSource() ```

description: Returns the title of the current page. @mcp
help: Returns the title of the current page. @mcp

description: Retrieves a lightweight version of the DOM tree (NanoDOMTree). @mcp
help:
Retrieves a lightweight version of the DOM tree (NanoDOMTree). @mcp

The NanoDOMTree is based on the accessibility tree and enhanced with DOM and document snapshot information. It is designed to be a compact representation suitable for AI processing and analysis.

description: Interact with an AI model using the context of the element selected by [selector]. @mcp
help:
Interact with an AI model using the context of the element selected by [selector]. @mcp

The AI model receives the element's content and structure as context along with the provided prompt.

description: Returns the cookies of the current page. @mcp
help:
Returns the cookies of the current page. @mcp

```kotlin val cookies = driver.getCookies() ```

description: Deletes browser cookies with matching name and url or domain/path pair. @mcp
help:
Deletes browser cookies with matching name and url or domain/path pair. @mcp

```kotlin driver.deleteCookies("name", "https://www.example.com") ```

> NOTE: At least one of the url and domain needs to be specified

match provided URL.

description: Clears browser cookies. @mcp
help:
Clears browser cookies. @mcp

```kotlin driver.clearBrowserCookies() ```

description: Wait until the element identified by the selector becomes present in the DOM or timeout. @mcp
help:
Wait until the element identified by the selector becomes present in the DOM or timeout. @mcp

```kotlin val remainingTime = driver.waitForSelector("h2.title") ```

description: Wait until the element identified by the selector becomes present in the DOM or timeout. @mcp
help:
Wait until the element identified by the selector becomes present in the DOM or timeout. @mcp

```kotlin val remainingTime = driver.waitForSelector("h2.title", 30000) ```

description: Wait for the element identified by the selector to become present in the DOM, or until timeout. @mcp
help:
Wait for the element identified by the selector to become present in the DOM, or until timeout. @mcp

```kotlin val remainingTime = driver.waitForSelector("h2.title", Duration.ofSeconds(30)) ```

description: Wait for the element identified by the selector to become present in the DOM, or until timeout. @mcp
help:
Wait for the element identified by the selector to become present in the DOM, or until timeout. @mcp

This method periodically checks for the existence of the element. If the element is not found during a check, the action will be executed, such as scrolling the page down.

```kotlin val remainingTime = driver.waitForSelector("h2.title") { driver.scrollDown() } ```

description: Wait for the element identified by the selector to become present in the DOM, or until timeout. @mcp
help:
Wait for the element identified by the selector to become present in the DOM, or until timeout. @mcp

This method periodically checks for the existence of the element. If the element is not found during a check, the action will be executed, such as scrolling the page down.

```kotlin val remainingTime = driver.waitForSelector("h2.title", 30000) { driver.scrollDown() } ```

description: Wait for the element identified by the selector to become present in the DOM, or until timeout. @mcp
help:
Wait for the element identified by the selector to become present in the DOM, or until timeout. @mcp

This method periodically checks for the existence of the element. If the element is not found during a check, the action will be executed, such as scrolling the page down.

```kotlin val remainingTime = driver.waitForSelector("h2.title", Duration.ofSeconds(30)) { driver.scrollDown() } ```

description: Wait until the current url changes or timeout. @mcp
help:
Wait until the current url changes or timeout. @mcp

```kotlin val url = "https://www.example.com" driver.navigate(url) var remainingTime = driver.waitForNavigation() if (remainingTime > 0) { driver.click("a[href='/next']") remainingTime = driver.waitForNavigation(url) } ```

description: Wait until the current url changes or timeout. @mcp
help:
Wait until the current url changes or timeout. @mcp

```kotlin val url = "https://www.example.com" driver.navigate(url) var remainingTime = driver.waitForNavigation(1000) if (remainingTime > 0) { driver.click("a[href='/next']") remainingTime = driver.waitForNavigation(url, 1000) } ```

description: Wait until the current url changes or timeout. @mcp
help:
Wait until the current url changes or timeout. @mcp

```kotlin val timeout = Duration.ofSeconds(30) val url = "https://www.example.com" driver.navigate(url) var remainingTime = driver.waitForNavigation(timeout) if (remainingTime > 0) { driver.click("a[href='/next']") remainingTime = driver.waitForNavigation(url, timeout) } ```

description: Await navigation to the specified URL page or timeout if necessary. @mcp
help:
Await navigation to the specified URL page or timeout if necessary. @mcp

```kotlin val newDriver = driver.waitForPage("https://www.example.com", Duration.ofSeconds(30)) ```

description: Wait until the predicate returns true. @mcp
help:
Wait until the predicate returns true. @mcp

```kotlin val remainingTime = driver.waitUntil { driver.exists("h2.title") } ```

description: Wait until the predicate returns true. @mcp
help:
Wait until the predicate returns true. @mcp

```kotlin val remainingTime = driver.waitUntil(10000) { driver.exists("h2.title") } ```

description: Wait until the predicate returns true. @mcp
help:
Wait until the predicate returns true. @mcp

```kotlin val remainingTime = driver.waitUntil(Duration.ofSeconds(10)) { driver.exists("h2.title") } ```

help:
Returns whether the element exists. @mcp

```kotlin driver.exists("h2.title") ```

description: Returns whether the element is hidden. @mcp
help:
Returns whether the element is hidden. @mcp

```kotlin driver.isHidden("input[name='q']") ```

help:
Returns whether the element is visible. @mcp

```kotlin driver.isVisible("input[name='q']") ```

help:
Returns whether the element is checked. @mcp

```kotlin driver.isChecked("input[name='agree']") ```

description: Brings the browser window to the front. @mcp
help:
Brings the browser window to the front. @mcp

```kotlin driver.bringToFront() ```

description: This method hovers over the element. @mcp
help:
This method hovers over the element. @mcp

1. Scroll the element into view if needed. 2. Move mouse to hover over a random position near the center of the element.

```kotlin driver.hover("h1") ```

description: This method fetches an element with `selector` and focuses it. @mcp
help:
This method fetches an element with `selector` and focuses it. @mcp

If there's no element matching `selector`, nothing to do.

```kotlin driver.focus("input[name='q']") ```

of an element to focus. If there are multiple elements satisfying the selector, the first will be focused.

description: This method emulates inserting text that doesn't come from a key press. @mcp
help:
This method emulates inserting text that doesn't come from a key press. @mcp

```kotlin driver.type("input[name='q']", "Hello, World!") ```

of an element to focus. If there are multiple elements satisfying the selector, the first will be focused.

description: This method emulates inserting text that doesn't come from a key press. @mcp
help:
This method emulates inserting text that doesn't come from a key press. @mcp

Unlike [type], this method clears the existing value before typing.

```kotlin driver.fill("input[name='q']", "Hello, World!") ```

of an element to focus, and then fill text into it. If there are multiple elements satisfying the selector, the first will be focused.

description: Shortcut for keyboard down and keyboard up. @mcp
help:
Shortcut for keyboard down and keyboard up. @mcp

The key is specified as a string, which can be a single character, a key name, or a combination of both. For example, 'a', 'A', 'KeyA', 'Enter', 'Shift+A', and 'Control+Shift+Tab' are all valid keys.

```kotlin driver.press("input[name='q']", "Enter") ```

of an element to focus, and then press a key. If there are multiple elements satisfying the selector, the first will be focused. See [Code values for keyboard events](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/UI_Events/Keyboard_event_code_values)

description: This method focuses an element with [selector] and clicks it. @mcp
help:
This method focuses an element with [selector] and clicks it. @mcp

If there's no element matching `selector`, nothing to do.

```kotlin driver.click("button[type='submit']") ```

of an element to focus. If there are multiple elements satisfying the selector, the first will be focused.

description: Focus on an element with [selector] and click it with [modifier] pressed. @mcp
help: Focus on an element with [selector] and click it with [modifier] pressed. @mcp

description: Focus on an element with [selector] and double-click it. @mcp
help: Focus on an element with [selector] and double-click it. @mcp

description: Focus on an element with [selector] and double-click it with [modifier] pressed. @mcp
help: Focus on an element with [selector] and double-click it with [modifier] pressed. @mcp

description: Resizes the viewport to the specified width and height. @mcp
help: Resizes the viewport to the specified width and height. @mcp

description: Accepts the dialog. @mcp
help: Accepts the dialog. @mcp

description: Dismisses the dialog. @mcp
help: Dismisses the dialog. @mcp

description: This method clicks an element with [selector] whose text content matches [pattern], and then focuses it. @mcp
help:
This method clicks an element with [selector] whose text content matches [pattern], and then focuses it. @mcp

If there's no element matching [selector], or the element's text content doesn't match [pattern], nothing to do.

```kotlin driver.clickTextMatches("button", "submit") ```

of an element to focus. If there are multiple elements satisfying the selector, the first will be focused.

description: This method clicks an element with [selector] whose attribute name is [attrName] and value matches [pattern], and then focuses it. @mcp
help:
This method clicks an element with [selector] whose attribute name is [attrName] and value matches [pattern], and then focuses it. @mcp

If there's no element matching [selector], or the element has no attribute [attrName], or the element's attribute value doesn't match [pattern], nothing to do.

```kotlin driver.clickAttributeMatches("button", "type", "submit") ```

of an element to focus. If there are multiple elements satisfying the selector, the first will be focused.

description: Selects one or more options in a <select> element. @mcp
help: Selects one or more options in a <select> element. @mcp

description: Selects an option in a <select> element. @mcp
help: Selects an option in a <select> element. @mcp

description: Clicks the nth anchor element in the DOM. @mcp
help:
Clicks the nth anchor element in the DOM. @mcp

This function searches for all anchor (`<a>`) elements within the specified root element, and clicks the nth anchor element (0-based index). If the anchor element exists, it returns the `href` attribute of the clicked anchor element. If the element does not exist, it returns null.

```kotlin driver.clickNthAnchor(100, "body") ```

description: This method check an element with [selector]. If there's no element matching [selector], nothing to do. @mcp
help:
This method check an element with [selector]. If there's no element matching [selector], nothing to do. @mcp

[selector](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_Selectors) of an element to check. If there are multiple elements satisfying the selector, the first will be checked.

description: This method uncheck an element with [selector]. If there's no element matching [selector], nothing to do. @mcp
help:
This method uncheck an element with [selector]. If there's no element matching [selector], nothing to do. @mcp

[selector](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/CSS_Selectors) of an element to uncheck. If there are multiple elements satisfying the selector, the first will be focused.

description: This method fetches an element with [selector], scrolls it into view if needed. @mcp
help:
This method fetches an element with [selector], scrolls it into view if needed. @mcp

If there's no element matching [selector], the method does nothing.

```kotlin driver.scrollTo("h2.title") ```

the [selector], the first will be selected.

description: The current page frame scrolls down for [count] times. @mcp
help:
The current page frame scrolls down for [count] times. @mcp

```kotlin driver.scrollDown(3) ```

description: The current page frame scrolls up for [count] times. @mcp
help:
The current page frame scrolls up for [count] times. @mcp

```kotlin driver.scrollUp(3) ```

description: Scrolls the current page frame vertically by the given number of pixels. @mcp
help:
Scrolls the current page frame vertically by the given number of pixels. @mcp

Positive values scroll down, negative values scroll up. The target position is clamped to the scrollable range [0, scrollHeight - viewportHeight].

When [smooth] is true a deterministic, step‑wise scrolling algorithm is used (instead of relying on the browser's native smooth scrolling) for higher stability: it issues incremental `window.scrollTo()` calls and waits for the viewport to settle after each step. This reduces race conditions with lazy‑loaded content or async layout changes and yields more predictable final positions.

Typical usage: ```kotlin // Scroll down 200px smoothly (default) driver.scrollBy() // Scroll up 500px immediately driver.scrollBy(-500.0, smooth = false) // Scroll down one viewport height, smooth val y = driver.scrollBy(driver.viewportHeight(), smooth = true) ```

`window.scrollTo()` is executed.

description: The current page frame scrolls to the top. @mcp
help:
The current page frame scrolls to the top. @mcp

```kotlin driver.scrollToTop() ```

description: The current page frame scrolls to the bottom. @mcp
help:
The current page frame scrolls to the bottom. @mcp

```kotlin driver.scrollToBottom() ```

description: The current page frame scrolls to the middle. @mcp
help:
The current page frame scrolls to the middle. @mcp

```kotlin driver.scrollToMiddle(0.2) driver.scrollToMiddle(0.5) driver.scrollToMiddle(0.8) ```

description: Scroll to the 2.5th viewport position. @mcp
help:
Scroll to the 2.5th viewport position. @mcp

```kotlin driver.scrollToViewport(1.0) driver.scrollToViewport(1.5) driver.scrollToViewport(2.5) driver.scrollToViewport(3.0) ```

1.00 means at the top of the first screen, 2.50 means halfway through the second screen.

description: The mouse wheels down for [count] times. @mcp
help:
The mouse wheels down for [count] times. @mcp

```kotlin driver.mouseWheelDown(3) ```

description: The mouse wheels up for [count] times. @mcp
help:
The mouse wheels up for [count] times. @mcp

```kotlin driver.mouseWheelUp(3) ```

help:
The mouse moves to the position specified by [x] and [y]. @mcp

```kotlin driver.moveMouseTo(100.0, 200.0) ```

description: The mouse moves to the element with [selector]. @mcp
help:
The mouse moves to the element with [selector]. @mcp

```kotlin driver.moveMouseTo("h2.title") ```

description: Performs a drag, dragenter, dragover, and drop in sequence. @mcp
help: Performs a drag, dragenter, dragover, and drop in sequence. @mcp

description: Returns the document's HTML markup. @mcp
help:
Returns the document's HTML markup. @mcp

```kotlin val html = driver.outerHTML() ```

If the document does not exist, returns null.

description: Returns the node's HTML markup, the node is located by [selector]. @mcp
help:
Returns the node's HTML markup, the node is located by [selector]. @mcp

If the node does not exist, returns null.

```kotlin val html = driver.outerHTML("h2.title") ```

description: Returns the text content of the document or a specific element. @mcp
help:
Returns the text content of the document or a specific element. @mcp

If [selector] is null, returns the text content of the entire document (usually `document.body.innerText`). If [selector] is provided, returns the text content of the first matching element.

If the document or element does not exist, returns null.

```kotlin val bodyText = driver.textContent() val titleText = driver.textContent("h1.title") ```

description: Extracts multiple fields from the page using CSS selectors. @mcp
help: Extracts multiple fields from the page using CSS selectors. @mcp

description: Use querySelectorAll to get all matched elements, and then return their NodeRefs. @mcp
help:
Use querySelectorAll to get all matched elements, and then return their NodeRefs. @mcp

Note that the NodeRefs may become stale after certain operations, so they should be used immediately after selection.

The type is determined by the prefix of the selector string, e.g. "css:div" for CSS selector, "xpath://div" for XPath, "e123" for backendNodeId. If no prefix is provided, CSS selector is used by default.

description: Returns the node's text content, the node is located by [selector]. @mcp
help:
Returns the node's text content, the node is located by [selector]. @mcp

If the node does not exist, returns null.

```kotlin val text = driver.selectFirstTextOrNull("h2.title") ```

description: Returns a list of text contents of all the elements matching the specified selector within the page. @mcp
help:
Returns a list of text contents of all the elements matching the specified selector within the page. @mcp

If no elements match the selector, returns an empty list.

```kotlin val texts = driver.selectTextAll("h2") ```

description: Returns the node's attribute value, the node is located by [selector], the attribute is [attrName]. @mcp
help:
Returns the node's attribute value, the node is located by [selector], the attribute is [attrName]. @mcp

If the node does not exist, or the attribute does not exist, returns null.

```kotlin val classes = driver.selectFirstAttributeOrNull("h2.title", "class") ```

description: Returns the node's attribute values, the node is located by [selector]. @mcp
help:
Returns the node's attribute values, the node is located by [selector]. @mcp

If the node do not exist, or the attribute does not exist, returns an empty list.

```kotlin val classes = driver.selectAttributes("h2.title", "class") ```

description: Returns the nodes' attribute values, the nodes are located by [selector], the attribute is [attrName]. @mcp
help:
Returns the nodes' attribute values, the nodes are located by [selector], the attribute is [attrName]. @mcp

If the nodes do not exist, or the attribute does not exist, returns an empty list.

```kotlin val classes = driver.selectAttributeAll("h2.title", "class") ```

description: Set the attribute of an element located by [selector]. @mcp
help:
Set the attribute of an element located by [selector]. @mcp

```kotlin driver.setAttribute("h2.title", "class", "header") ```

description: Set the attribute of all elements matching the CSS query. @mcp
help:
Set the attribute of all elements matching the CSS query. @mcp

```kotlin driver.setAttributeAll("h2.title", "class", "header") ```

help:
Returns the node's property value, the node is located by [selector], the property is [propName]. @mcp

If the node does not exist, or the property does not exist, returns null.

```kotlin val classes = driver.selectFirstPropertyOrNull("input#input", "value") ```

description: Returns the nodes' property values, the nodes are located by [selector], the property is [propName]. @mcp
help:
Returns the nodes' property values, the nodes are located by [selector], the property is [propName]. @mcp

If the nodes do not exist, or the property does not exist, returns an empty list.

```kotlin val classes = driver.selectPropertyAll("input#input", "value") ```

help:

help:
Set the property of all elements matching the CSS query. @mcp

Find anchor elements matching the CSS query. @mcp

help:

description: Executes JavaScript in the context of the currently selected frame or window. @mcp
help:
Executes JavaScript in the context of the currently selected frame or window. @mcp

If the result is not JavaScript object, it is not returned.

If you want to execute a function, convert it to IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression).

```kotlin val title = driver.evaluate("document.title") ```

Multi-line JavaScript code:

```kotlin val code = """ () => { const a = 10; const b = 20; return a * b; } """.trimIndent()

val result = driver.evaluate(code) ```

### 🔍 Notes: * **Wrap the code in an IIFE (Immediately Invoked Function Expression)** to return a value. * **Escape line breaks** with `\n`.

description: Executes JavaScript and returns the result, or [defaultValue] if the result is null or incompatible. @mcp
help: Executes JavaScript and returns the result, or [defaultValue] if the result is null or incompatible. @mcp

description: returns detailed evaluation metadata (beta). @mcp
help: returns detailed evaluation metadata (beta). @mcp

description: Executes JavaScript and returns the result as a JSON-serializable value. @mcp
help:
Executes JavaScript and returns the result as a JSON-serializable value. @mcp

If the result is an object, it is serialized to a Map or List. If the result is a primitive, it is returned as is. If the result is null or undefined, null is returned.

description: Executes JavaScript and returns the result as a JSON-serializable value, or [defaultValue] if null/incompatible. @mcp
help: Executes JavaScript and returns the result as a JSON-serializable value, or [defaultValue] if null/incompatible. @mcp

description: Returns detailed value evaluation metadata (beta). @mcp
help: Returns detailed value evaluation metadata (beta). @mcp

description: Capture a screenshot of the current viewport (or primary browsing context) after ensuring any pending layout. @mcp
help:
Capture a screenshot of the current viewport (or primary browsing context) after ensuring any pending layout. @mcp

If the backend supports element-centric capture this may represent the full page; implementation specific.

The target element (if any) is scrolled into view before capture.

```kotlin val base64 = driver.screenshot() val bytes = Base64.getDecoder().decode(base64) ```

description: Scroll the element matched by [selector] into view (if needed) then take a screenshot of that element's bounding box. @mcp
help:
Scroll the element matched by [selector] into view (if needed) then take a screenshot of that element's bounding box. @mcp

Returns a Base64 encoded image (implementation usually PNG/JPEG), PNG by default.

description: Take a screenshot of the rectangle specified by [rect] in the current page coordinate space. @mcp
help:
Take a screenshot of the rectangle specified by [rect] in the current page coordinate space. @mcp

Caller is responsible for ensuring the rectangle is visible or scrolled into view if the implementation requires it.

description: Calculate the clickable point of an element located by [selector]. @mcp
help:
Calculate the clickable point of an element located by [selector]. @mcp

If the element does not exist, or is not clickable, returns null.

description: Return the bounding box of an element located by [selector]. @mcp
help:
Return the bounding box of an element located by [selector]. @mcp

If the element does not exist, returns null.

description: Create a new Jsoup session with the last page's context, which means, the same headers and cookies. @mcp
help: Create a new Jsoup session with the last page's context, which means, the same headers and cookies. @mcp

description: Load the url as a resource with Jsoup rather than browser rendering, with the last page's context. @mcp
help:
Load the url as a resource with Jsoup rather than browser rendering, with the last page's context. @mcp

This means, the same headers and cookies.

description: Load the url as a resource without browser rendering, with the last page's context. @mcp
help:
Load the url as a resource without browser rendering, with the last page's context. @mcp

This means, the same headers and cookies.

description: Force the page pauses all navigations and PENDING resource fetches. @mcp
help:
Force the page pauses all navigations and PENDING resource fetches. @mcp

If the page loading pauses, the user can still interact with the page, and therefore resources can continue to load.

description: Force the page stop all navigations and RELEASES all resources. @mcp
help:
Force the page stop all navigations and RELEASES all resources. @mcp

Interaction with the stop page results in undefined behavior and the results should not be trusted.

If a web driver stops, it can later be used to visit other pages.
